name: Shop
description: A transaction interface where players buy, sell, or exchange items and resources through priced offers.
design_notes: A shop is a promise that price, stock, ownership, and wallet changes will resolve predictably. Good shops make offer_id, stock_refresh_rule, purchase_limit, price lookup, sellback_ratio, and rollback behavior clear enough that players trust the transaction.
design_purpose: Turn currency and resources into deliberate item, upgrade, or recovery choices.
Create pacing through stock limits, location access, refresh timing, and price curves.
Provide a visible sink for rewards and a fallback path for players who missed random drops.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are choosing the right purchase from a reliable merchant or store system.

parameters.name: vendor_id
parameters.description: Identifier for shop stock, pricing rules, access requirements, and refresh state.

parameters.name: offer_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the purchasable listing used in UI, validation, and telemetry.

parameters.name: stock_refresh_rule
parameters.description: Rule that replenishes or rotates stock for the vendor.

parameters.name: purchase_limit
parameters.description: Maximum quantity of an offer the player can buy within the defined scope.

parameters.name: sellback_ratio
parameters.description: Fraction of item value returned when the player sells to this shop.

edge_cases: A player buys the last stock item while another client or save slot has the same shop open.
The vendor refreshes between quote and commit, invalidating offer_id or purchase_limit state.
A purchase grants an item_instance_id but inventory capacity rejects it after currency has been debited.
Sellback of upgraded, bound, or damaged items must decide how much metadata affects price.
common_bugs: Shop UI disables a buy button based on stale wallet data and allows or blocks the wrong purchase.
Currency is debited before stock validation, so failed purchases remove coins.
Offer IDs are regenerated every refresh and old saves or pending purchases cannot reconcile them.
Sellback duplicates items because item removal and currency grant are not one transaction.
balancing_notes: Stock limits and refresh cadence can be more important than price for controlling access to powerful items.
Shops should not sell every missing reward cheaply unless the intended role is catch-up or anti-frustration.
Sellback ratios protect experimentation, but high ratios can erase the cost of wrong purchases.
accessibility_notes: Show affordability, owned count, purchase limit, stock remaining, and item comparison without requiring fast menu navigation.
Add confirmation or undo for expensive purchases, especially when cursor focus can move during controller input.

implementation_notes.unity: Process purchases through a shop transaction service: validate vendor_id, offer_id, stock version, price quote, wallet debit, inventory capacity, item_instance_id grant, and save commit before playing success feedback.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep shop UI nodes read-only; send buy/sell requests to an Autoload economy service that owns stock state, purchase_limit counters, and wallet mutations.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a replicated vendor actor or economy subsystem for stock and pricing; server RPCs should reject stale offer_id versions and roll back if item grant or currency debit fails.
implementation_notes.web: Represent purchase as a transaction object containing quote_id, offer_id, currency diff, stock diff, and item grant so optimistic UI can be corrected cleanly.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses shops during runs for temporary purchases and recovery.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda
example_games.note: Uses shops as readable sources for consumables and equipment.
